prevalence.trend: increasing awareness and diagnosis; trauma exposure remains high in some settings

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: trauma exposure with intrusive symptoms, avoidance, negative mood/cognition changes, and hyperarousal lasting >1 month with impairment

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; trauma-focused psychotherapy access is limited

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often diagnosed in specialized services; APS screening is limited
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Trauma-focused psychotherapy (CBT/EMDR) and SSRIs when indicated; management of comorbid depression/substance use
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to trauma-focused psychotherapy and high social exposure to violence in some communities
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: CAPS and psychosocial networks can provide longitudinal support where available
